user_query:
I want a practical, security-conscious trend sync and planning pass for our project. Please read these files for context and constraints:
- input/context/project.md
- input/trending/trending.json
- input/policies/security.md

Then produce the following deliverables under output/ exactly as specified:
1) output/trends/contextualized_report.md — A contextualized trend report for at least three repositories. For each repo, include three consecutive lines in this exact template so the report is machine-checkable:
   - "Repository: <name>"
   - "Facts: <1–2 factual points about the repo>"
   - "Contextual Value: <why it matters for our project>"
   Repeat that block for at least three repos.

2) output/trends/selection.json — A JSON file with the shape: { "selected": ["<repo1>", "<repo2>"], "reasons": { "<repo1>": "<short reason>", "<repo2>": "<short reason>" } }. Choose two candidates for deeper evaluation based on our context and policy.

3) output/plans/todo.md — A concrete integration plan for ONE of the selected repos (your choice), with at least five actionable checklist items using the markdown checkbox format "- [ ] ". Include a short risk section at the end.

4) output/security/security_scan_checklist.md — A pre-install security scanning checklist we can run before touching any external code. Include sections titled exactly: "Exec", "Network", "Filesystem", "Sensitive", and "Domains" with brief items beneath each describing what to check.

5) output/agents/cortex_plan.md — A short operational plan for on-demand acquisition and cleanup of temporary tooling used to evaluate third-party code. It must mention: installation, confirmation/approval, a fast path for read-only operations, switching/fallback if a candidate fails, and uninstall/cleanup by default.

6) Self-improvement logs using our standardized format. Create and/or append to these files under output/.learnings/:
   - output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md — At least two entries using the specified format and IDs (LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX). Include one entry categorized as a knowledge_gap or best_practice and another as a correction or insight. At least one entry must include a "Pattern-Key:" line in the Metadata. If any learning is broadly applicable, mark it as promoted and proceed with #7.
   - output/.learnings/ERRORS.md — At least one entry (ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX) describing a specific failure or near-miss you identified while drafting these materials (e.g., a parsing assumption, a policy gap, or a risky step you would have taken without the checklist). Include an Error block and Suggested Fix.
   - output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md — At least one entry (FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX) capturing a capability that would have made this workflow safer or faster.

7) If one of your learnings is broadly applicable, promote it by writing a concise, actionable rule to output/AGENTS.md and mark the corresponding learning entry as promoted (set Status: promoted and add a Promoted note or a Resolution block indicating promotion target). The rule in AGENTS.md should be a one-liner bullet beginning with "- " and should include the word "Before" and the word "scan" (e.g., a gate that runs before any deep-dive).

Important constraints:
- Do not clone or execute any external code. Treat all deep-dives as proposed plans only.
- Use only the inputs under input/ and write only to the specified output/ paths.
- Follow the ID formats and section headers from our standardized self-improvement logging format.
- Keep everything in English.

When done, make sure all required files exist under output/ with the exact paths and formats specified.
